---
title: "Coils, Orbits, And Closure"
---

A coil is the orbit traced by repeatedly applying the same rotation. Closure means the repeated motion has returned to its starting address.

The previous lesson moved once. This lesson asks what happens when the same stride is used again and again. The visited addresses form an orbit. Because `C_n` has only finitely many nodes, the motion eventually repeats; the first return to the starting node is the period of that stride.

This is the first Circle Calculus pattern that scales upward: choose a space, choose a motion, follow the repeated motion, then study how and when it closes.

<div class="warning-box">
The widget output is a finite computation. It helps explain the theorem but is not a formal proof.
</div>

## Picture

<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="CC-0201"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="CC-0202"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="CC-0205"></div>

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0004"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0005"></div>

<div class="widget-panel" data-widget="coil_orbit_explorer" data-theorem-id="CC-T0005" data-dictionary-ids="CC-0201 CC-0202 CC-0205"></div>

## Try It

Start with `n = 12`, stride `4`, and start `0`. Write down the orbit before reading the widget output. Then switch the stride to `5` and compare how much of the circle is visited.

::: {.checkpoint-box}

## Checkpoint

What is the difference between a node being visited by an orbit and the orbit being closed?

:::

## Source Trail

Python reference model:

```python
from circle_math.finite import Circle

C = Circle(12)
C.orbit(start=0, stride=4)  # [0, 4, 8]
C.period(4)                # 3
```

<p data-paper-ref="papers/PAPER_01_FINITE_CIRCLES.md">Paper source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/PAPER_01_FINITE_CIRCLES.md">Circle Calculus I</a></p>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
<script type="module" src="../../widgets/S1/coil_orbit_explorer.js"></script>
